chunks.text: Jerome A. Feldman is a Professor Emeritus at EECS, UC Berkeley. Contact information includes 739 Soda Hall, phone 510-666-2900, fax 666-2956, and email jfeldman@cs.berkeley.edu. Links provided are to his personal homepage, publications, and dissertations.

Jerome A. Feldman's education includes a Ph.D. in Computer Science and Mathematics from Carnegie Mellon University in 1964, an M.S. in Mathematics from the University of Pittsburgh in 1961, and a B.S. in Physics from the University of Rochester in 1960.

His research areas are Artificial Intelligence (AI), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Security (SEC), and cognitive science. He is associated with research centers including the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR) and the Berkeley Center for New Media (BCNM).

Selected publications include 'Language Understanding and Unified Cognitive Science' (2007), 'From Molecule to Metaphor: A Neural Theory of Language' (2006), and 'A (Somewhat) New Solution to the Binding Problem' (2006). Other works cover topics like embodied semantics and connectionist models from 2004 to 2002.

Additional publications include 'Embodied meaning in a neural theory of language' (2004), 'A Connectionist Encoding of Parameterized Schemas and Reactive Plans' (2002), and earlier works like 'Four frames suffice: A provisional model of vision and space' (1985) and 'Translator writing systems' (1968).

Selected honors and awards include the Berkeley Citation in 2009, Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) in 2005, and Fellow of the Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AAAI) in 1990.
